title: Confidence-calibrated basis gating

queries: graph soft prompt confidence gating alignment residual graph llm
graph prompt projection confidence calibration basis residual gating structured evidence

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: Graph-text transfer quality depends on the prompt-side projection interface, so safer prompt injection can matter as much as adding stronger supervision.
papers.relevance: Supports modifying basis residual injection itself, not only its auxiliary losses, when alignment signals have become unstable across tasks.

papers.title: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Graph Question Answering
papers.claim: Compressed graph prompts lose fidelity when noisy evidence is injected without enough selectivity.
papers.relevance: Supports making prompt-side basis injection more selective instead of uniformly mixing every residual into every prompt token.

papers.title: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
papers.claim: Graph-enhanced generation quality depends on how structural evidence is organized and exposed to the language model, not only on encoder strength.
papers.relevance: Supports a confidence-aware gating bundle as a structure-preserving interface change after sampling-side heuristics failed globally.

evidence.why_now: EXP039 and EXP054 showed that removing graph residual injection helps user-ltv but is not globally safe, while EXP042 showed that simple graph-side rebalance still regresses user-churn. EXP072 then showed that switching to prompt-side sampling alone can make all three representative tasks worse. The next evidence-backed branch is therefore to keep the existing basis channel but make residual injection conditional on basis posterior confidence.

hypothesis: The current basis path likely over-injects low-confidence residuals into prompt tokens, which preserves some classification-side structure but destabilizes regression and salt-side ranking. Scaling token and graph residual mixing by the sharpness of the inferred basis posterior should keep confident basis transfers alive while damping noisy graph-side perturbations, making this a safer global candidate than either constant graph residuals or more aggressive sampling heuristics.
notes: This candidate returns to the basis construction / injection layer after the first bridge_route sampling bundle failed globally.
Implementation scope is intentionally narrow: keep the existing basis losses and residual paths, but scale token- and graph-side residual mixing by basis posterior confidence.
Do not add pretrain, do not revisit the saturated route-aware conservative loss family, and do not relaunch near-duplicate sampling heuristics while this bundle is active.
Launched on 2026-05-07 after syncing the updated main.py/model.py gating patch plus generated wrappers and launcher to the remote repo and /tmp.
Selected currently idle GPUs at launch time: user-churn on GPU 0, user-ltv on GPU 5, item-incoterms on GPU 6.
First orchestrator status check shows all three windows up and logs at remote DB-load stage.
Completed on 2026-05-07 with global verdict: failed.
Confidence-calibrated gating reduced the size of the churn regression versus EXP072, but all three representative tasks still remained below the refreshed 512-step strict baselines.
